title: Unit, Property, Mutation, Headless, GUI, and Play Tests · Program Brief: Playtest Protocol
summary: Tests assert internal lists, fake a green result, require the developer display, and never prove a deliberate defect is detected. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the playtest protocol increment in layered maze verification suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define representative tasks, accommodations, observation boundaries, barrier severity, consent, privacy, and closure evidence.
Use pure fakes and Tcl-only checks where valid, then run Tk and display tests under a controlled platform matrix.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-playtest-protocol — Define representative tasks, accommodations, observation boundaries, barrier severity, consent, privacy, and closure evidence.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-testing-properties-headless-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-testing-properties-headless-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_testing_properties_headless_4(model_tests: int = 96, property_cases: int = 240, killed_mutants: int = 12, surviving_mutants: int = 0, native_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if model_tests < 24 or property_cases < 64:
        return False, model_tests, "test-depth"
    if killed_mutants < 8:
        return False, killed_mutants, "mutation-coverage"
    if surviving_mutants > 0:
        return False, surviving_mutants, "undetected-defect"
    if not native_gates_named:
        return False, property_cases, "headless-overclaim"
    assert surviving_mutants == 0
    return True, killed_mutants, "layered-maze-verification"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-playtest-protocol-1wgfuf
# Competency: maze-playtest-protocol.
# Case 6119: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: event-loop work must remain cancellable.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_playtest_protocol_1wgfuf(model_tests: int = 96, property_cases: int = 240, killed_mutants: int = 12, surviving_mutants: int = 0, native_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1wgfuf = "1wgfuf-fufgw1-6119"
    assert evidence_variant_1wgfuf
    evidence_axes_1wgfuf = (
        "grid1wgfuf",
        "topology6119",
        "searchfufgw1",
        "event1wgfuf6119",
        "access61191wgfuf",
        "recoveryfufgw16119",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1wgfuf)) == 6
    if model_tests < 24 or property_cases < 64:
        return False, model_tests, "test-depth"
    if killed_mutants < 8:
        return False, killed_mutants, "mutation-coverage"
    if surviving_mutants > 0:
        return False, surviving_mutants, "undetected-defect"
    if not native_gates_named:
        return False, property_cases, "headless-overclaim"
    assert surviving_mutants == 0
    return True, killed_mutants, "layered-maze-verification"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define representative tasks, accommodations, observation boundaries, barrier severity, consent, privacy, and closure evidence.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Playtest Protocol model · Theory Playtest Protocol, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Playtest Protocol precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
